text: Git branching is an essential part of modern version control. When you work on a project, you can create separate feature branches to develop new ideas without affecting the main code. Once your work is complete, you merge your branch back into the main branch. A common workflow involves creating a feature branch, making changes, and then opening a pull request for team review. This allows others to check your code before merging it. Overall, using branches helps keep your version control workflow organized, reduces conflicts, and makes collaboration safer and more efficient.
